---
title: "Example Code"
format: revealjs
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished presentation. To learn more about Quarto presentations see <https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/>.

## Bullets

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes:

-   Content authored with markdown
-   Output from executable code

## Code

When you click the **Render** button a presentation will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:


# Setup

```{r}
# Load Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(plotly)
library(reshape2) #https://rpubs.com/OwaisAli/1103477
library(readxl) 
```

Creating my signature theme
```{r}
my_theme<- theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#FBFAF8", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#FBFAF8", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#E8E3DC", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.title = element_text(family = "sans", color = "#4A4A4A"),
    axis.text  = element_text(color = "#6A6A6A"),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 19, color = "#2F2F2F", hjust = 0.3),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "#6F6F6F", hjust = 0.25),
    plot.caption = element_text( size = 9, color = "#8A8A8A", hjust = -1),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", family = "sans"),
    legend.background = element_blank(),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA)
  )
```


```{r}



kSCt4 <- read_excel("kSCt4.xls")

approval <- kSCt4|>
  ggplot( aes( x= modeldate) ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = disapprove_lo, ymax = disapprove_hi, fill = '#16877a'),
    alpha=0.35
  )+
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = approve_lo, ymax = approve_hi, fill = '#d96003' ),
    alpha=0.35
  )+
  geom_line(
    aes( y= disapprove_lo ),
    color= '#d96003',
    linetype = 'dotted',
    size = 0.8,
    alpha = 0.6
  )+
  geom_line(
    aes( y= disapprove_hi ),
    color= '#d96003',
    linetype = 'dotted',
    size = 0.8,
    alpha = 0.6
  )+
  geom_line(
    aes( y= approve_lo ),
    color= '#16877a',
    linetype = 'dotted',
    size = 0.8,
    alpha = 0.6
  )+
  geom_line(
    aes( y= approve_hi),
    color= '#16877a',
    linetype = 'dotted',
    size = 0.8,
    alpha = 0.6
  )+
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 50
  )+
  geom_line(
    aes( y= disapprove ),
    color= '#d96003',
    alpha = 1,
    size = 0.8
  )+
  geom_line(
    aes( y= approve ),
    color= '#16877a',
    alpha = 1,
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = kSCt4 |>
      slice_tail(n = 1),
    aes(x = modeldate, y = disapprove, label = paste0("Disapprove\n", round(disapprove, 1), "%")),
    color = '#d96003',
    size = 3.5,
    hjust = -0.1,
    vjust = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = kSCt4 |>
      slice_tail(n = 1),
    aes(x = modeldate, y = approve, label = paste0("Approve\n", round(approve, 1), "%")),
    color = '#16877a',
    size = 3.5,
    hjust = -0.1,
    vjust = 0.8
  ) +
  labs(
    title= "Do Americans <span style = 'color: #16877a ;'>approve </span>or <span style = 'color: #d96003 ;'>dispprove</span> of
Donald Trump?",
    subtitle = "An updating polling average of Donald Trump's approval rating and disapproval rating in his
second term, accounting for each poll's quality, recency, sample size, and partisan lean.",
    caption = "Note: 90% of polls are projected to fall within the shaded regions.
Updated February 17, 2026 at 11:04 PM . Get the data . Download image
") +
  scale_x_date(
    limits = c(as.Date("2025-01-20"), as.Date("2026-02-11")),
    breaks = seq(as.Date("2025-04-01"), as.Date("2026-01-01"), by = "3 months"),
    labels = c("Apr 2025", "Jul", "Oct", "Jan 2026"),
    expand = expansion( mult = c(0, .1))
  )+
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(30,70),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, ".0%"),
    expand = expansion( mult = c(0, .1))
  )+
  guides(fill = "none", color = "none")  +
  annotate("label",
           x = as.Date("2025-03-05"),
           y = 70,
           label = "Approval/disapproval",
           fill = "black",
           color = "white",
           label.size = 0,
           label.r = unit(0.3, "lines"),
           fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("label",
           x = as.Date("2025-05-05"),
           y = 70,
           label = "Net approval",
           fill = "white",
           color = "black",
           label.size = 0.5,
           label.r = unit(0.3, "lines"),
           fontface = "bold") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    #plot.title = element_markdown(size = 30, hjust = 0, face = "bold", family = "sans"), (element)
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 20, family = "sans"),plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#f2f2f2", color = NA),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.caption.position = "plot",
    plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "italic", family = "sans"))

```

Interactive

```{r}
ggplotly(approval) |>
 layout(
  title= list(
    text = paste0(
     "Do Americans <span style='color:#16877a'>approve</span> or <span style='color:#d96003'>disapprove</span> of Donald Trump?",
     "<br>",
      "<sub>An updating polling average of Donald Trump's approval rating and disapproval rating in his<br>second term, accounting for each poll's quality, recency, sample size, and partisan lean</sub><br>"
     ),
     x = 0,
    xanchor = "left",
    y = 0.97,
    yanchor = "top")
 )
```
